description.description: description is a free-form text that may be used in various cases like in the Helm chart, the Dockerfile, etc.

options.description:
options to add for CI/CD generation.
Those are various and may differ depending on used CI/CD provider.

local.description: local (in the repository) renovate configuration path to use in global extends.

license.description:
license can be one of the available options with GitLab license API.

With this property, kickr will automatically download the appropriate license file and save it in the project.

maintainers.description:
maintainers defines the list of project maintainers.

When using kickr, at least one maintainer must be given.
Maintainers may be used in various places like in the Helm chart, Dockerfile, Goreleaser configuration, etc.

platform.description:
platform defines the source code hosting platform (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Bitbucket).

By default, it will be guessed based on Git remote URL.
However, in case of mirroring, or other specific cases (like custom hosting), it may be updated (and it won't be overridden) to provide a custom platform.

The platform serves in various cases to generate the right files format (dependabot for instance) but also for badges in README.md.

pre-commit.description:
pre-commit section can be provided to add additional, non-default, checks with pre-commit.

All options available here are implemented in kickr templates, there's no possibly to directly give pre-commit hooks definition.

In case a specific hook, not implemented, must be provided, then '.pre-commit-config.yaml' file can be modified
and the top comment indicating that it's generated can be removed to avoid being overridden.

deploy.description: deploy helm chart to kubernetes cluster(s).

environments.description:
environments to associate a specific Kubernetes context to a branch.

Only those four environments can be provided, defining under the hood specific behaviors (for GitHub Actions):
  - 'review' will only be run on non-protected branches
  - 'integration' will only be run on protected
  - 'staging' will only be run on the default branch
  - 'production' will only be run on the default branch

Concerning GitLab CI/CD, rules can be found at https://gitlab.com/to-be-continuous/helm#managed-deployment-environments.

path.description: path within the helm registry (default is the path on VCS remote URL).

publish.description: publish helm chart to the registry.

registry.description:
registry URL (both OCI and non-OCI based registry are supported).

Depending on the project registry (GitHub, Jfrog, Nexus, etc.), this property may need a more specific path like 'oci://jfrog.example.com/my/registry'.

directory.description: directory where is located the website to deploy (default is '.').

auth.description:
auth corresponds to the authentication method used by semantic-release when creating GitHub / GitLab releases.

Since GitLab provides features around tokens, this property cannot be given when running with it (i.e. will always be 'personal-token').

options.description: options to add in release job / release configuration.
